name: Tour Aotearoa - North Island
description: The North Island section of Tour Aotearoa with varied surfaces and strong route support.
difficultyExplanation: The distance is big, but the North Island TA has frequent towns and established route support. It is demanding over time rather than highly technical day to day.
beAwareOf: Weather changes, wind exposure, and changing trail conditions can all materially affect daily progress.
tips: Start early, carry a weather margin, and keep backup navigation plus an extra food and water buffer.
trickiestSection: The Timber Trail adds technical singletrack midway through the leg. Most riders find the long Forgotten Highway gravel section hardest to pace mentally.
routeSummary: Tour Aotearoa - North Island is a intermediate bikepacking route covering about 1,639 km, commonly ridden over 14 days. The North Island section of Tour Aotearoa with varied surfaces and strong route support. Riders can expect 55% Gravel / 25% Road / 20% Track as the route unfolds.

Good planning makes this route far more enjoyable. The best riding window is generally Nov-Mar, with resupply described as Carry enough food and water for longer gaps between settlements; verify opening hours before committing to remote sections.. Overnight options are typically A mix of camping, huts, and local stays depending on your pace and daily target distance..

The key section to manage is: The Timber Trail adds technical singletrack midway through the leg. Most riders find the long Forgotten Highway gravel section hardest to pace mentally. Keep a close eye on Weather changes, wind exposure, and changing trail conditions can all materially affect daily progress., especially if conditions change during the day.
recommendedSetupText: For this 14-day route, we recommend a Touring Bike to balance comfort and control across mixed terrain. This setup keeps key gear stable and easy to access over long days.
